extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won the sprint after sprint teams shut down a three-rider break with 15km remaining, beating CIMOLAI and MERLIER on the uphill finish.
tactical_patterns: PELLAUD took maximum KOM points from break despite inferior GC position, revealing aggressive small-team jersey hunting.
SAGAN contested intermediate sprint from peloton while in breakaway-chase mode, prioritizing ciclamino over energy conservation.
Sprint teams caught break at 15km despite block headwind, suggesting coordinated tempo control beyond typical catch zones.
GAVIRIA launched with 500m on uphill finish but faded, confirming disadvantage of early sprint initiation on gradient finales.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: won 2nd stage from positioned sprint on uphill finish, showing gradient versatility beyond pure flat speed
rider_signals.VALTER Attila: neutral: survived first pink jersey defense on flat stage with strong Quick-Step protection before mountain weekend
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: timed sprint 500m out on uphill finish and completely faded, tactical error or lack of kick
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: neutral: suffered mechanical inside final kilometers on sprint stage, positioning suggests GC vigilance even on flat terrain
